name: Phase 2 N-terminal anti-tau antibody in PSP (naive, no differentiation)
population: Probable PSP-Richardson syndrome per MDS-PSP 2017 criteria
eligibility: Age 40-80; established PSPRS at screening; able to walk 5 steps unaided

intervention.mechanism: binds extracellular N-terminal tau to block trans-synaptic seeding and spread
intervention.stated_differentiation: First-in-class opportunity in PSP; we dose higher than prior attempts and expect a larger effect.

primary_endpoint: Change from baseline in PSP Rating Scale (PSPRS, 28-item) at 52 weeks

powering.assumed_effect: 30% slowing of PSPRS progression vs placebo
